god_files.evidence: ~85 top-level functions, zero section comments. Mixes at least 6 distinct concerns: case-report orchestration (_case_report*, 3 variants), diff/comparison engine (_line_differences, _field_issue, _issue, _amount_selection_evidence, _highest_severity), a receipt<->statement reconciliation/matching simulator (_simulate_reconciliation, _merchant_similarity, _normalize_merchant, _score_line_candidate, _date_bounds, _amount_tolerance), artifact I/O (_write_case_artifacts + 2 manifest variants, _write_json/_load_json), 6 separate payload-example builders (_matched/_statement_only/_receipt_only/_ambiguous/_manual_review/_failed_payload_example), promotion/comparison logic (_deterministic_comparison, _promotion_recommendation, _entry_better), and markdown rendering (_markdown_report). This is the largest file in the repo and the clearest true-monolith.
god_files.split_candidate: reconciliation/matching engine -> statement/reconciliation.py; diffing -> statement/diff.py; artifact I/O -> statement/artifacts.py; payload example builders -> statement/payload_examples.py; markdown rendering -> statement/markdown.py

god_files.evidence: Docstring says 'Runtime statement layout routing and deterministic PDF parsing' but the file actually houses two parallel, mostly-independent extraction pipelines for two statement families: CMR-issuer parsing (_cmr_candidate_row + unprefixed default helpers _row/_transaction_date_word/_selected_amount_word/_amount_candidates) and bank-issuer parsing, which has its own fully mirrored set of 10 _bank_*-prefixed functions (_bank_candidate_row, _bank_transaction_date_word, _bank_selected_amount_word, _bank_line_currency, _bank_description, _bank_original_amount, _bank_amount_candidates, _bank_unselected_amount_role, _bank_amount_column_label, _bank_amount_selection_reason) spanning lines 531-895. Zero section comments mark the boundary; only the naming convention does.
god_files.split_candidate: extract the _bank_* pipeline into statement_routing_bank.py and the _cmr_/default pipeline into statement_routing_cmr.py, keeping route_statement_layout + shared word/row extraction (extract_statement_with_pymupdf, _extract_words, _group_words_into_rows) as the shared entry file

god_files.evidence: Docstring: 'Unknown statement fallback using compact rows plus a Gemini layout profile' — a single pipeline (evidence building -> profile application -> row/date/amount/installment candidate extraction -> description/currency selection) for one strategy (LLM-derived layout profile for issuers statement_routing.py doesn't recognize). Long because of many small single-purpose extraction helpers, not because of mixed concerns.

Lives in prompt_lab (confirmed an offline dev-tool CLI package, not imported by the runtime app/api or app/services — only backend/app/services/scan_test_cases.py touches prompt_lab). Despite the name overlap with statement_profile_fallback.py, this is an evaluation/calibration-report generator (build_statement_fallback_calibration_report, mismatch %, prompt-improvement candidates, markdown report) that consumes outputs of the runtime fallback, not a duplicate of its logic. Single concern: offline calibration reporting.

Lines 9-1663 (97% of the file) are a flat messages object literal with es/en/pt translation-key blocks (each ~550 lines); only ~40 lines at the end are actual logic (isSupportedLocale, negotiateLocale, getPreferredLocale, setPreferredLocale, translate). High churn (60 commits/90d) is expected/healthy — every new UI string touches all 3 locale blocks. Splittable into es.ts/en.ts/pt.ts + logic.ts but that's a stylistic nicety, not urgent debt.

One async scan-processing state machine (acquire -> stage1 -> stage2 -> transition/complete/fail/requeue). A minor split seam exists: the e2e-fixture and mock-provider test-support pipeline (_run_e2e_fixture_pipeline, _run_mock_provider_pipeline, _fail_e2e_fixture_scan, _throttle_fixture_scan, lines 624-830, ~200 lines) exists purely to support e2e tests and could move to a scan_worker_test_support.py, but the core is a single cohesive pipeline.

The HomeScreen component itself is ~235 lines (106-341); the remaining 640 lines are five presentational subcomponents (ImagePreview, ScanProgress, StageRow, ScanResult, ScanError) plus small formatters, all specific to the home screen's scan-capture flow — not unrelated screens bundled together. Easily splittable into per-component files but is a single coherent feature (home + scan flow), not a mixed-concern monolith.

Pure declarative reference data: a CategoryDefinition dataclass plus a large table of _category(...) calls building the canonical 4-level (L1-L4) category taxonomy with es/en/pt display labels. No branching logic beyond the small builder helper. Long because the taxonomy is broad, not because concerns are mixed.
Docstring: 'GENERATED by scaffold_config.py' — a config data file (SITE dict + doc-entry list) feeding the gabe-docsite generator. Not hand-maintained code.

Header: 'This file was auto-generated by openapi-typescript. Do not make direct changes to the file.' Produced by npm run generate:api (web/package.json, mobile/package.json) from backend/app/main.py's OpenAPI spec, and re-verified for drift in scripts/pre-push.sh ('web · api-types up to date', 'mobile · api drift' checks).

Byte-identical (diff exit 0) to web/src/lib/api-types.d.ts, and web/src/lib/openapi-spec.json is byte-identical to mobile's copy too — both are independent codegen outputs of the same backend OpenAPI spec via each app's own generate:api script, not a hand-maintained duplicate.

frontend/ last commit 2026-05-31 (5+ weeks stale vs. daily commits elsewhere). .github/workflows/ci.yml lines 15-17 explicitly state CI validates 'the DEPLOYED web app (web/), not the retired legacy frontend/'. Confirmed zero external imports (grep -rln 'from.*frontend/' across web/, mobile/, backend/ returns nothing) and no infra/firebase deploy target references frontend/dist.
Same abandoned frontend/ tree as App.tsx above; not imported by web/ or mobile/, no active build target.

Same abandoned frontend/ tree. web/ has its own live, independently-evolved SankeyChart.tsx at web/src/components/charts/SankeyChart.tsx (252 lines) — the dead frontend/ copy is not a source anything currently depends on.
Same abandoned frontend/ tree, no external references found.

duplication.what: Small regex/constant vocabulary re-implemented independently rather than shared: both files define their own date/installment/amount/currency regexes tuned to their own input model (word-position tokens in statement_routing.py vs raw text spans in statement_profile_fallback.py). This is NOT full-logic duplication — the two files implement genuinely different strategies (deterministic known-issuer geometric parser vs LLM-profile-guided fallback for unknown issuers) — but the primitive regex constants (_DATE_RE, _INSTALLMENT_RE, money/amount regex) could live in one shared statement_patterns module.

api-types.d.ts (5631 lines) and openapi-spec.json (10025 lines) exist byte-identically in both web/ and mobile/. Verified as intentional generated codegen output (openapi-typescript, backed by generate:api scripts and a pre-push drift check), not hand-copied duplication — each app runs its own codegen from the same backend spec because web/ and mobile/ are separate deployables with separate toolchains/node_modules. Flagging only as a candidate for a shared package if the drift-check overhead ever becomes a maintenance cost.

design-lab's SankeyChart.tsx (553 lines, Storybook/mock-data reference) and web's production SankeyChart.tsx (252 lines) differ substantially (684-line diff) — this is the expected design-lab-to-web 'port' divergence noted as by-design in the audit brief, not unhealthy duplication. Included here only to record that the check was performed and no drift concern was found.

dead_code.what: The entire frontend/ tree (React 18 app) is abandoned in favor of web/ (React 19 + Vite 8). CI comment in .github/workflows/ci.yml explicitly calls it 'the retired legacy frontend/'; last commit to frontend/ was 2026-05-31 vs. same-day commits to web/ and mobile/ during this audit. No file outside frontend/ imports anything from it.
dead_code.evidence: git log -1 -- frontend/ => 2026-05-31; .github/workflows/ci.yml:15-17 comment; grep -rln 'from.*frontend/' web/ mobile/ backend/ => empty; only stale references left are in docs, .kdbp archives, and two housekeeping scripts (scripts/ralph-housekeeping.sh, scripts/check-prod-bundle.sh) that are themselves not wired into CI (check-prod-bundle.sh is not invoked from any workflow or script).

dead_code.what: scripts/check-prod-bundle.sh defaults to checking frontend/dist and is not called by any CI workflow or other script in the repo — dead tooling pointed at a dead tree.
dead_code.evidence: grep -rn 'check-prod-bundle' .github/workflows/*.yml scripts/*.sh => only self-reference in its own usage comment; no invocation found.

verdict_reasoning: Most of the alarming line counts are explained by benign or expected causes: two files are verified generated codegen (api-types.d.ts x2, openapi-spec.json x2), one is a generated config (docsite.config.py), several are pure reference data or single-concern pipelines that are merely long (categories.py, i18n.ts, statement_profile_fallback.py, fallback_calibration.py, scan_worker.py, HomeScreen.tsx), and four of the largest (App.tsx, translations.ts, SankeyChart.tsx, DrillDownGrid.tsx, totaling ~5,400 lines) sit in the confirmed-abandoned frontend/ tree that a normal repo cleanup (not a recurring lifecycle pass) would simply delete. Two files are genuine true-monoliths with clearly identifiable mixed concerns and zero section-comment scaffolding: report.py (3214 lines, ~85 functions spanning report orchestration, diffing, reconciliation matching, artifact I/O, and markdown rendering) and statement_routing.py (1368 lines, two fully parallel CMR/bank extraction pipelines under one roof). The suspected 3-way fallback-logic duplication does not hold up — statement_routing.py, statement_profile_fallback.py, and fallback_calibration.py are three different layers (known-issuer deterministic parse, unknown-issuer LLM-profile fallback, offline calibration reporting) that share domain vocabulary but not logic. Net effect: a one-time cleanup (delete frontend/, split report.py, split statement_routing.py by issuer family) would resolve essentially everything found; there is no evidence of a broad, self-perpetuating complexity pattern across the codebase that would keep generating comparable monoliths, so a recurring lifecycle 'simplify' step is not justified by this evidence — a scoped one-time pass is.

god_files.evidence: Exports 4 unrelated top-level screens from one file: NotificationsScreen (L231), DishHistoryScreen (L239, with its own DishHistoryFilterPanel/buildDishHistoryFilterFacets/DishHistoryTimelineFeed cluster ~L1142-1600), IngredientHistoryScreen (with its own parallel IngredientHistoryFilterPanel/buildIngredientHistoryFilterFacets/IngredientHistoryTimelineFeed cluster ~L1789-2334), and SettingsScreen (L245, with an embedded SettingsSubscriptionPanel ~L2567-2701 and facet-config machinery ~L2701-3220). Each cluster has its own facet builders, selection-state helpers, and timeline renderers with almost no shared code between them.
god_files.split_candidate: DishHistoryScreen.tsx (+its filter panel/timeline), IngredientHistoryScreen.tsx (+its filter panel/timeline), NotificationsScreen.tsx, SettingsScreen.tsx + SettingsSubscriptionPanel.tsx

god_files.evidence: 72 commits/90d — the single highest-churn file in the whole codebase. Correctly composes the shared @design-system/organisms/FilterFlow primitives (FilterOptionGrid, FilterPanelHeader wrapped as RecipeFilterHeader L954) rather than reimplementing them, so this is NOT an R1 violation. But it still bundles 6+ independently-shaped facet implementations in one file: IngredientFacetContent (L1201)+IngredientCategoryRail (L1267), OriginFacetContent (L1359), SaborFacetContent (exported, L1792)+SaborIntensityPanel/SaborBarRow, ComplexityFacetContent (exported, L1931), MinutesRangeFacetContent (exported, L2070), EquipmentFacetContent (L1493)+EquipmentAddConfirm, plus a separate saved-filter-lookup persistence concern (L2260-2330) and the facet-taxonomy builder buildRecipeFilterFacets (L2332-2557, 225 lines by itself).
god_files.split_candidate: Per-facet files (IngredientFacetContent.tsx, OriginFacetContent.tsx, SaborFacetContent.tsx, ComplexityFacetContent.tsx, MinutesRangeFacetContent.tsx, EquipmentFacetContent.tsx) + a separate savedFilterLookup.ts + buildRecipeFilterFacets.ts

god_files.evidence: Interleaves 4 distinct kinds of content: (a) type/interface definitions L18-750ish, (b) taxonomy constant option arrays + getters (cookingRecipeTypeOptions, SABOR/TEXTURA/TEMPERATURA_CODE_LABELS, cookingTechniqueOptions, etc.) L463-925, (c) large mock/seed data arrays (cookingRecipes L1271-1708, cookingNowTasks, multiDayCookingTasks, defaultPreparedCookingHistoryItems) L1114-1791, and (d) pure business logic unrelated to taxonomy/mock data — filterCookingRecipes (L1932), portion clamping (L2195-2221), scaleCookingIngredientAmount (L2221), getCookingIngredientInventory + pantry-location badge/stock logic (L2254-2366), and measurement-unit normalization/parsing (L2373-2450).
god_files.split_candidate: types.ts, taxonomy.ts (option lists+getters), mockData.ts (seed arrays), recipeFiltering.ts, ingredientScaling.ts/measurementUnits.ts

god_files.evidence: Single exported function (RecipeBrowseContainer, L145) whose ~1140-line body is a god-function: 60 internal useState/useMemo/useCallback declarations. Its own header comment (L1-27) enumerates at least 6 separate wiring responsibilities bolted into one component: recipe catalog + pantry-availability query, the 4-mode Explore query, recipe-detail query, cooking-session POST flow (create session, allergen 403 handling), the prepared-history rail (GET /history/dishes), and long-prep stage-reminder polling/CAS-advance. Correctly reuses queryStateToScreenState (shared with PantryContainer.tsx, 262 lines) so this is a genuine scope/size problem, not a missing-abstraction one.
god_files.split_candidate: useRecipeCatalog, useExploreModes, useRecipeDetailFlow, useCookingSessionStart, useHistoryRail, useStageReminders — one hook per concern already named in the file's own comment header

god_files.evidence: Only 3 exports (RecipeDetailScreen L204, RecipeModeReadOnlyStepList L1688, RecipeModeStartChoiceOverlay L2050) — one genuinely complex screen (recipe detail + cooking-mode walkthrough: mode picker, step checklist, timers, photo capture, chef/practitioner qualification, start/session payload builders), not several unrelated screens. Not a true-monolith, but large enough that the step-checklist+timer machinery (~L1799-2138) and the session/payload builders (~L2437-2512) are self-contained and could move to their own hook/module without touching the render tree.
god_files.split_candidate: extract step-checklist+timer state into a useRecipeModeSteps hook; extract session/payload builders into recipeModeSession.ts

god_files.evidence: Single export (CookingScreen, L250). All other functions are unexported helpers serving that one screen's states (idle/loading/error/empty/first-time/recipes/planned/active) and two layouts (DeviceCookingLayout L447, DesktopCookingLayout L592). It is one cohesive Cooking-home screen, not multiple screens stitched together, but the per-state renderers (RecipesState L937, PlannedCookingPreview+PlannedCookingBucket L1623-1930, ActiveCookingPreview L1517) are large, self-contained, and low-risk to extract.
god_files.split_candidate: move RecipesState, PlannedCookingPreview/Bucket, ActiveCookingPreview each to their own sibling file

god_files.evidence: 6 files (RecipeFilterPlayground.archive.tsx 2884, RecipeCreationPlayground.archive.tsx 2544, RecipeDetailModePlayground.archive.tsx 2291, RecipeCardLayoutPlayground.archive.tsx 1559, features/settings/spikes/archive/DishHistoryPlayground.archive.tsx 1616, IngredientHistoryPlayground.archive.tsx 968) verified via grep to have zero importers anywhere in apps/web/src and zero .stories.tsx siblings in their archive folders — i.e., genuinely unreferenced. This matches R4 ('Archive inactive spikes out of active Storybook') by design, but the code still sits ~9.9K lines in the active source tree rather than being deleted.
god_files.split_candidate: n/a — candidate for deletion, not splitting

god_files.evidence: File header: '/** This file was auto-generated by openapi-typescript. Do not make direct changes to the file. */'. Regenerated by scripts/gen_api_types.sh from the FastAPI OpenAPI spec and enforced frozen/additive-only by scripts/check_openapi_frozen.sh in CI (regenerates from shared/openapi.snapshot.json and diffs the symbol surface).
File header: '# generated — do not edit manually. Populated by scripts/gen_taxonomy.py (Batch 2). Source: shared/taxonomy.json'.

scripts/gen_icon_registry.py (docstring L3-10) explicitly regenerates the `generatedIcons` literal in this file ('MOCKUP_TS = ... mockupAssets.ts', with a stale-check + regeneration routine at L130-139 of the script) alongside generatedCatalogIcons.ts.
File header: '// GENERATED by scripts/gen_icon_registry.py — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND (Phase 49 / D96.6).'

16 related exports, all serving one feature area (the post-cook 'log' screen): rating (CookingLogRating L238), technique stats (L357), pantry-discount panel (L208), mode panel (L149), recipe summary (L124), plus storage-location helper functions (L18-56). Single concern, no mixed/unrelated screens found.

Only 1 main export (PantryScreen, L145). Carries 2 dead legacy-alias exports (`miCocinaStates`, `MiCocinaScreen` = PantryScreen, L1324-1325) verified via grep to have zero importers elsewhere in apps/web/src — small dead code, otherwise a single cohesive screen.

~30 functions/2 classes, all recipe-domain service logic: 14 filter-condition builders (allergen/diet/technique/taste/texture/temperature, L194-499), _apply_recipe_filters (L500-774), then CRUD/availability/planning/equipment/allergen service functions (L775-1428). Cohesive around one domain, but the filter-condition builders are a natural low-risk extraction.
god_files.split_candidate: recipe_filters.py (the 14 condition builders + _apply_recipe_filters) split from the CRUD/planning service functions

god_files.evidence: FastAPI router, ~12 @router route handlers for the single 'recipes' resource family (list/explore/detail/patch/plan/unplan/equipment/create/gustify-generate), plus 2 small response-shaping helpers. Large but each handler is bounded; one resource, one router file is a defensible convention.

duplication.what: Byte-identical age-in-days helper duplicated instead of shared: getDishHistoryAgeInDays and getIngredientHistoryAgeInDays have the exact same 5-line body (Date.parse both sides, floor of the 86_400_000ms diff). This is one instance of a broader pattern — Dish History and Ingredient History in the same file independently re-implement ~9 parallel-shaped functions (facet-selected-options, facet-selected-ids, update-selection, applied-filter-summary, applied-filter-tokens, clearable-filter-count, get-deduped-older-items) with the same shape but no shared abstraction.

Identical dietary-tag lookup helper independently reimplemented in two feature files instead of being exported once from the shared taxonomy module (CookingScreenModel.ts already exports the plural/localized getCookingDietaryTagOptions, but not this exact singular non-localized lookup).

The same 5 pantry-location display labels (Despensa/Refrigerador/Congelador/Estante cocina/Mesón) are independently hardcoded in two different feature-model files — once as the canonical pantry location catalog, once as a separate 'pantryLocationBadges' object built inside the cooking domain model purely to render ingredient-source chips. A label/icon change requires editing both files with no shared source of truth or compile-time link between them.

dead_code.what: ~9,862 lines across 6 '*.archive.tsx' Storybook spike files with zero imports and zero .stories.tsx entries anywhere in the codebase.
dead_code.evidence: grep -rln for each of RecipeFilterPlayground.archive, RecipeCreationPlayground.archive, RecipeDetailModePlayground.archive, RecipeCardLayoutPlayground.archive, DishHistoryPlayground.archive, IngredientHistoryPlayground.archive across apps/web/src (excluding their own /archive/ folder) returned no matches; the archive folders also contain no .stories.tsx files, confirming they are not even reachable via Storybook.

dead_code.what: Legacy alias exports `MiCocinaScreen` and `miCocinaStates` in PantryScreen.tsx (L1324-1325) have zero importers.
dead_code.evidence: grep -rln "MiCocinaScreen|miCocinaStates" across apps/web/src excluding PantryScreen.tsx itself returned no matches.

verdict_reasoning: Of the 6 highest-priority/highest-churn files, 4 are genuine true-monoliths with concretely nameable split seams (ProfileScreens.tsx bundles 4 unrelated screens; RecipeBrowseContainer.tsx is a single 1140-line function wiring 6 distinct concerns its own comments enumerate; CookingScreenModel.ts interleaves types+taxonomy+mock-data+business-logic; RecipeFilterPanel.tsx is the single highest-churn file in the repo at 72 commits/90d despite being correctly factored against the shared FilterFlow design system). The other 2 (CookingScreen.tsx, RecipeDetailScreen.tsx) are honestly cohesive-but-long rather than monoliths. Three independently file-verified duplication instances (byte-identical getDishHistoryAgeInDays/getIngredientHistoryAgeInDays, duplicated getDietaryTagOption, and pantry-location labels hardcoded twice across CookingScreenModel.ts and PantryLocationModel.ts) show the same 'grow a concept locally instead of sharing it' pattern recurring inside these exact hotspot files. Separately, ~9,862 lines of *.archive.tsx spike code are verified fully dead (zero imports, zero stories) and still sit in the active tree. About 9,300 of the pre-measured 'over-cap' lines are correctly generated/frozen build artifacts (api-types.ts, taxonomy.py, mockupAssets.ts, generatedCatalogIcons.ts) and should stay excluded from any simplify scope. Given the high churn rates on the true-monolith files (29-72 commits/90d — these are actively being extended, not legacy/frozen code), a one-time cleanup would regress again as new facets/screens/fields keep landing in the same files; a recurring simplify pass has real, repeated work to do.
